topic: wine alcohol and chili heat perception
trigger_keywords: alcohol en la percepción de picante en la comida

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A wine with noticeable alcohol is consumed with food containing chili heat.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Alcohol adds its own warming sensation and can intensify the irritation produced by capsaicin rather than neutralising it.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The food can feel hotter and the wine's alcohol more burning, making the pairing less comfortable.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: The food can feel hotter and the wine's alcohol more burning, making the pairing less comfortable.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
